report:
BRAIN MRI
Axial and sagittal SE T1-weighted, axial and coronal TSE T2-weighted and axial FLAIR, axial DWI and ADC mapping, weighted sequences were obtained. IV contrast agent was not used.

In sections passing through the infratentorial region, the fourth ventricle is midline and of normal size. Brainstem structures and basal cisterns are within normal limits. Signal intensities of the cerebellar hemispheres are normal.
In sections passing through the supratentorial region, the third and lateral ventricles are midline and of normal size.
Bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Cerebral gray matter structures and sulcal structures are within normal limits. Intracranial herniation was not detected.
One or two millimetric foci with hyperintense characteristics on T2-weighted and FLAIR images, showing no diffusion restriction on diffusion-weighted images, are observed in the right periventricular white matter and left centrum semiovale.

Diffusion restriction was not detected on diffusion-weighted images. No signal change was observed on the SWI sequence.

Impression: One or two millimetric hyperintense foci in the right periventricular white matter and left centrum semiovale

report:
Clinical information: Compared with brain MRI dated [date_1].
BRAIN MRI
Axial and sagittal plane spin echo T1 weighted, axial and coronal plane TSE T2 weighted and axial plane FLAIR weighted, axial plane DWI and ADC mapping weighted; coronal, sagittal and axial T1 weighted sequences obtained after IV contrast agent.

In slices passing through the infratentorial region, the fourth ventricle is midline and of normal width. Basal cisterns are within normal limits. Signal intensities of the cerebellar hemispheres are normal.
In slices passing through the supratentorial region, the third and lateral ventricles are midline and of normal width.
Bilateral basal ganglia, thalami are normal. Cerebral gray matter formations and sulcal structures are within normal limits. Intracranial herniation was not detected.
Multiple MS plaques located in the posterior portion of the periventricular white matter of both sides, in the left mesencephalon, in the body of the bilateral corpus callosum, and appearing as several black hole-like foci in the bilateral centrum semiovale persist without significant difference in number and intensity.
In the left centrum semiovale, apart from faintly marginated contrast enhancement in delayed contrast images, active plaque enhancement was not observed. Contrast enhancement patterns did not show significant difference compared to the previous examination. In the right subcortical white matter, in the plaque that showed significant contrast enhancement in early and delayed contrast slices in the previous examination, no enhancement is observed in the current examination.
Diffusion restriction was not detected in diffusion-weighted images. No signal change was recorded in the SWI sequence.

Impression: Patient with MS diagnosis, plaques in the described areas persist without significant difference. Acute plaque was not detected.
CERVICAL MRI
Sagittal plane TSE T1 and TSE T2 weighted, axial plane at C2-3, C3-4, C4-5, C5-6 and C6-7 intervertebral discs ME 2D weighted, coronal, sagittal and axial T1 weighted sequences obtained after IV contrast agent.

Cervical lordosis is flattened.
Integrity of cervical vertebral body heights and neural arch elements is normal.
Signal loss due to degeneration is present in the cervical intervertebral discs. Demyelinating plaque was not detected.
Herniation finding was not detected in the cervical intervertebral discs.
Intensity and width of the cervical spinal cord, craniocervical junction are normal.
Anteroposterior diameter of the cervical spinal canal and neural foramina are normal.
Lesion showing contrast uptake after IV contrast agent was not recorded.

Impression:
Flattening in cervical lordosis, signal loss due to degeneration in cervical intervertebral discs

PATIENT NAME: [patient_1]
EXAMINATION DATE: [date_2]
EXAMINATION REGION: Contrast-Enhanced Cranial MRI Examination
Protocol No: [accession_1]
Clinical information: Headache

Technique:
-Sagittal plane 5 mm slice thickness FSE T2, axial plane 5 mm slice thickness FSE T1, T2 and diffusion, coronal plane 5 mm slice thickness FLAIR weighted slices were obtained. After contrast injection axial plane 3D FSE gradient T1, axial plane 1 mm thickness 3D FSE gradient T1 and coronal plane 1 mm thickness fat-sat 3D FSE gradient T1 weighted slices were added (15 cc Clariscan was used).

Findings:
	Nasopharynx is observed as normal and parapharyngeal fat planes are open. Morphology and signal intensities of parotid glands entering the slice plane are normal. Clivus signal is observed as normal. Sellar and parasellar structures are normal. No craniocervical junction anomaly was detected.

  	Signal distributions of cerebellar hemispheres are observed as normal and width of cerebellar cortical sulci is normal. Medulla oblongata, pons and midbrain morphology and signal intensities are normal. Fourth ventricle is in normal form and location. Prepontine and cerebellopontine cisterns are open. Signal distributions of cisternal and canalicular segments of 7-8 nerve complexes are normal.

Supratentorial area midline structures are in normal location and ventricular system width and morphology are normal. Signal distributions of bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Gray-white matter signal distribution in supratentorial brain parenchyma is normal. Widths of cortical sulci are normal. Corpus callosum morphology and signal intensity are normal. No pathological contrast uptake was detected in infra and supratentorial brain parenchyma.

Aeration of paranasal sinuses is normal. Bilateral globes and retrobulbar fat planes appear normal. Morphology and signal intensities of optic chiasm and optic nerves are normal. Flow signal patterns of intracranial major vascular structures are observed as normal. No pathological significant signal change was detected in the calvarium.

Impression:
*Contrast-enhanced cranial MRI examination within normal limits.

BRAIN MRI EXAMINATION

TECHNIQUE: Axial T2-weighted, Axial T1-weighted, Axial-FLAIR, sagittal T1-weighted, coronal T2-weighted

FINDINGS:

The bilateral cerebellar hemispheres, pons, medulla oblongata, and medulla oblongata demonstrate normal radiomorphology and homogeneous signal intensity.
The fourth ventricle is midline located and of normal width.
Both internal acoustic canals are symmetric and of normal width; cranial nerves VII and VIII are of physiological appearance.
The craniocervical junction appears normal.
Bilateral trigeminal nerves are observed to be normal.
The depths of the cerebral convexity sulci and fissures are normal.
The configuration and signal intensities of the gray-white matter structures are physiological.
The thickness and signal intensity of the corpus callosum appear normal.
The aqueductal opening and CSF flow are normal; the pineal gland is observed to be physiological.
The third ventricle is midline. No midline shift is detected in the midline structures.
Bilateral lateral ventricles are normal.
The basal ganglia and bilateral thalami location and intensities are normal.
The sella turcica width and pituitary gland are observed to be normal. The cavernous sinuses appear normal.
The cerebral dural sinuses and the arterial structures of the circle of Willis are observed to be normal.
Bilateral globes, orbital muscles, optic nerves, and retrobulbar fatty tissues are normal; no pathological change is observed in intra- or extraconal locations.
The skull base, cranial bone structures, diploe width, and the radiomorphology and signal intensities of the dural structures appear normal.
Polypoid-type mucosal thickening is observed in the right maxillary sinus.
Both inferior turbinates are hypertrophic. There is minimal deviation to the left in the superior aspect of the nasal septum.

IMPRESSION: Brain MRI examination within normal limits.

-Sagittal SE T1 slices with 5 mm slice thickness, axial FSE PD T2-weighted slices with 5 mm slice thickness, and coronal FSE T2-weighted, FLAIR, and volumetric gradient T1-weighted slices with 3 mm slice thickness directed towards temporal horns were obtained.

Signal distributions of the cerebellar hemispheres appear normal and cerebellar cortical sulcal widths are normal. Medulla oblongata, pons, and midbrain morphology and signal intensities are normal. Fourth ventricle is normal in form and localization. Pre pontine and cerebellopontine cisterns are patent. Signal distributions of the cisternal and canalicular segments of the 7th and 8th nerve complexes are normal. Flow signal patterns of the intracranial vertebral arteries and basilar artery are normal. Dural sinuses are patent. No tonsillar herniation was detected.
Midline structures in the supratentorial region are in normal localization and ventricular system width and morphology are normal. Signal distributions of the bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Gray-white matter signal distribution in the supratentorial brain parenchyma is normal. Cortical sulcal widths are normal. Corpus callosum morphology and signal intensity are normal. Morphologies and signal intensities of the bilateral hippocampi and parahippocampal gyri are normal.
Aeration of the paranasal sinuses is normal. Bilateral globes and retrobulbar fat planes appear normal. Morphology and signal intensities of the optic chiasm and optic nerves are normal. Flow signal patterns of the major intracranial vascular structures appear normal. No pathological significant signal change was detected in the calvarium.

Impression:
*Cranial MRI examination within normal limits.
*No findings suggestive of mesial temporal sclerosis were detected.

Impression: Contrast-enhanced cranial MRI findings within normal limits.

report:
BRAIN MRI (CONTRAST-ENHANCED)

Clinical information: Headache, fever, body pain, meningitis?
Technique: Axial T2 TSE, DWI, SWI, Coronal T1 IR, T2 TSE, sagittal T2 TSE, 3D FLAIR, 3D T1 MPRAGE, post-contrast 3D T1 MPRAGE (Clariscan 5 ml was used in the examination).

Findings:

Medulla oblongata, pons, midbrain, bilateral cerebellar hemispheres, and vermis parenchyma are normal.

Fourth ventricle and basal cisterns are normal.

Bilateral thalami, basal ganglia, internal and external capsules, and corpus callosum are observed to be normal.

Periventricular white matter, corona radiata and centrum semiovale are normal.

Cerebral gyral pattern and cerebral cortex are normal.

Amygdala, hippocampus and fornices are normal bilaterally.

No specific features were detected on diffusion-ADC and SWI sections.

Intracranial pathological enhancement was not detected.

Major intracranial arterial vascular structures and dural venous sinuses appear patent.

Third ventricle and both lateral ventricles are of normal size. Hemispheric cortical sulcal widths are compatible with the patient's age.

No pathology was observed in the sellar, parasellar, and suprasellar structures.

Both orbits, optic nerves, optic chiasm and optic tracts appear normal. Aeration of the paranasal sinuses is normal. Nasopharyngeal air column is patent. Bilateral middle ear and mastoid cell aeration is normal.

Imaged bony structures are normal.

Impression:

• Brain MRI examination within normal limits.

TECHNIQUE: 
-5 mm slice thickness FSE T2 in the sagittal plane, 5 mm slice thickness FSE T1, T2, and diffusion in the axial plane, and FLAIR-weighted slices in the coronal plane with 5 mm slice thickness were obtained. Following contrast injection, additional axial 3D FSE gradient T1, axial 3D FSE gradient T1 with 1 mm thickness, and coronal fat-sat 3D FSE gradient T1-weighted slices with 1 mm thickness were added (15 cc Clariscan was used).

FINDINGS:
	The nasopharynx appears normal, and parapharyngeal fat planes are patent. Morphology and signal intensities of the parotid glands within the field of view are normal. The clivus signal is normal. Sellar and parasellar structures are normal. No craniocervical junction anomaly was detected.  

	Signal distribution of the cerebellar hemispheres is normal, and the widths of the cerebellar cortical sulci are normal. Morphology and signal intensities of the medulla oblongata, pons, and mesencephalon are normal. The fourth ventricle is of normal form and location. Prepontine and cerebellopontine cisterns are patent. Signal distribution of the cisternal and canalicular segments of the 7th-8th cranial nerve complexes is normal.  

	Midline structures in the supratentorial compartment are in normal location, and the ventricular system width and morphology are normal. Signal distribution of the bilateral basal ganglia and thalamus is normal. A millimetric linear T2 hyperintense signal is observed in the deep white matter of the right parietal lobe, and pathological contrast enhancement was not detected. Thought to be related to a Virchow-Robin space. Aside from this, the supratentorial brain parenchymal signal is normal. The widths of the cerebral cortical sulci are normal. 

	Aeration of the paranasal sinuses is normal. Bilateral globes and retrobulbar fat planes appear normal. Morphology and signal intensities of the optic chiasm and optic nerves are normal. Flow signal patterns of the intracranial major vascular structures are normal. No pathological signal change was detected in the calvarium.  

IMPRESSION:
*A millimetric linear T2 hyperintense signal is observed in the deep white matter of the right parietal lobe, and pathological contrast enhancement was not detected. Thought to be related to a Virchow-Robin space.

Clinical information: Syncope.
Technique: Axial T2 TSE, DWI, SWI, Coronal T1 IR, T2 TSE, Sagittal T2 TSE, 3D FLAIR, 3D T1 MPRAGE, postcontrast 3D T1 MPRAGE (6 ml Clariscan was used).

Findings: 

The medulla oblongata, pons, midbrain, bilateral cerebellar hemispheres, and vermis parenchyma are normal. The fourth ventricle and basal cisterns are normal.

The bilateral thalami, basal ganglia, internal and external capsules, and corpus callosum are observed as normal. Periventricular white matter, corona radiata, and centrum semiovale are normal. The cerebral giral pattern and cerebral cortex are normal. The amygdala, hippocampus, and fornices are normal bilaterally. 

No specific finding was detected on diffusion-ADC and SWI sections. 

Intracranial pathological enhancement was not detected.

Major intracranial arterial vascular structures and dural venous sinuses are patent. 

The third ventricle and both lateral ventricles are of normal size. Hemispheric cortical sulcal widths are compatible with the patient's age. 

No pathology was observed in the sellar, parasellar, and suprasellar regions.

Both orbits, optic nerves, chiasm, and optic tracts appear normal. The cisternal parts of the bilateral 3rd, 5th, and 6th cranial nerves, and the cisternal and intracanalicular parts of the bilateral 7th-8th nerve complexes are normal. The bilateral internal acoustic canals, cochlea, and vestibule appear normal. Bilateral middle ear and mastoid cell aeration is normal. Paranasal sinus aeration is normal. The nasopharyngeal air column is patent. 

Visualized osseous structures are normal. 

Impression: 

•	Brain MRI examination within normal limits.

report:
BRAIN (CRANIAL) MRI
Clinical information: Headache, blurred vision.
Technique: 3-plane T1-weighted, FLAIR; axial T2-weighted, SWI, and DWI.
Findings:
Brainstem, cerebellum, and cerebral parenchyma; ventricles and sulci are normal.
No abnormalities are observed in DWI and SWI sections.
Major cerebral vascular structures are patent and normal. The hyperintensity observed on axial FLAIR section 27 is a slow-flowing vein within the hypoglossal canal.
A fat-suppressed 8 x 6 mm diameter lipoma is present at the exit of the right stylomastoid foramen (T1-weighted coronal 98, axial 27).
A small right sphenoid retention cyst is present, and paranasal sinuses, middle ear aeration, nasopharynx, and orbits are normal.
No abnormalities are observed in the visualized bones.

Impression: Cranial MRI findings within normal limits; 8 mm diameter lipoma at the exit of the right stylomastoid foramen.

report:
BRAIN MRI
Axial and sagittal plane SE T1, axial and coronal plane TSE T2 and axial plane FLAIR, axial plane DWI and ADC mapping, weighted sequences were obtained. IV contrast agent was not used.

In slices passing through the infratentorial region the fourth ventricle is midline and of normal width. Brainstem structures and basal cisterns are within normal limits. Signal intensities of the cerebellar hemispheres are normal.
In slices passing through the supratentorial region the third and lateral ventricles are midline and of normal width.
The bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Cerebral gray matter structures and sulcal structures are within normal limits. Intracranial herniation was not detected.
In the bilateral periventricular white matter and centrum semiovale multiple lesions showing hyperintense on T2-weighted and FLAIR images, showing no diffusion restriction on diffusion-weighted images were observed.
No signal change was observed on SWI sequence.

Impression:
Multiple hyperintense lesions in the bilateral periventricular white matter and centrum

report:
BRAIN MRI AND MR ANGIOGRAPHY
Clinical information: Headache
Technique: 3 planes T1-weighted, FLAIR; axial T2-weighted, SWI, and DWI. T1-weighted after IV 10 ml Gadovist injection. MRA with TOF technique directed at the circle of Willis.
Findings:
Brainstem, cerebellum, and cerebral parenchyma; ventricles and sulci are normal.
No specific findings on DWI, SWI, and post-contrast sections.
On MRA examination, bilateral ICA, MCA, and ACA diameters are normal, and contours are smooth. Bilateral VAs, basilar artery, PICA, SCA, and right PCA are normal. The left PCA P1 segment is congenitally hypoplastic, and the p2 and distal segments are filled from the PComA.
Bilateral maxillary retention cysts are present, and paranasal sinuses and middle ear aeration, nasopharynx, and orbits are normal.
No specific findings in visualized bones.
Impression: Cranial MRI and MR Angiography findings within normal limits; left PCA P1 hypoplasia.

report:
BRAIN MRI
Axial and sagittal SE T1-weighted, axial and coronal TSE T2-weighted, and axial FLAIR, axial DWI and ADC mapping weighted sequences were obtained. IV contrast agent was not used.

On slices passing through the infratentorial region, the 4th ventricle is midline and of normal width. Brainstem structures and basal cisterns are within normal limits. Signal intensities of the cerebellar hemispheres are normal. A distinct nodular formation measuring 1 cm in diameter is present between the clivus and axis (belonging to atlas bone ?).
On slices passing through the supratentorial region, the 3rd and lateral ventricles are midline and of normal width.
Bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Cerebral gray matter structures and sulcal structures are within normal limits. Intracranial herniation was not detected.
On diffusion-weighted images, diffusion restriction was not detected. On SWI sequence, signal change was not recorded.

Loss of aeration is present in the right mastoid cells.

At the vertex level, several nodular lesions located in the skin and subcutaneous tissue, the largest measuring 3 cm in diameter in the right parietal region, compatible with sebaceous cysts with dense contents, were observed.

Impression:
Right mastoiditis?
Nodular lesions compatible with sebaceous cysts located in the skin and subcutaneous tissue of the scalp. Evaluation recommended in conjunction with clinical and physical examination findings.
Nodular formation between clivus and axis (belonging to atlas bone ?, evaluation recommended with cervical CT).

- Sagittal plane 5 mm slice thickness FSE T2-weighted, axial plane 5 mm slice thickness FSE T1-weighted, T2-weighted and diffusion, coronal plane 5 mm slice thickness FLAIR weighted slices were obtained. Following contrast injection, axial plane 3D FSE gradient T1-weighted, axial plane 1 mm thickness 3D FSE gradient T1-weighted and coronal plane 1 mm thickness fat-sat 3D FSE gradient T1-weighted slices were additionally obtained (15 cc Clariscan was used).

FINDINGS:
	Nasopharynx is observed to be normal and parapharyngeal fat planes are clear. Morphology and signal intensities of the parotid glands visualized within the slice plane are normal. Clivus signal is observed to be normal. Sellar and parasellar structures are normal. Craniocervical junction anomaly was not detected.

	Signal distributions of the cerebellar hemispheres are observed to be normal and widths of the cerebellar cortical sulci are normal. Medulla oblongata, pons, and mesencephalon morphology and signal intensities are normal. Fourth ventricle is of normal form and location. Prepontine and cerebellopontine cisterns are clear. Signal distributions of the cisternal and canalicular segments of the 7th-8th nerve complexes are normal.

Supratentorial area midline structures are in normal location and ventricular system width and morphology are normal. Signal distributions of the bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Gray-white matter signal distribution in the supratentorial brain parenchyma is normal. Widths of the cortical sulci are normal. Corpus callosum morphology and signal intensity are normal. Pathological contrast enhancement was not detected in the infratentorial and supratentorial brain parenchyma.

Aeration of the paranasal sinuses is normal. Bilateral globes and retrobulbar fat planes appear normal. Morphology and signal intensities of the optic chiasm and optic nerves are normal. Flow signal patterns of the intracranial major vascular structures are observed to be normal. No pathologically significant signal change was detected in the calvarium.

IMPRESSION:
*Contrast-Enhanced Cranial MRI Examination within normal limits.

report:
BRAIN MRI
Axial and sagittal SE T1, axial and coronal TSE T2, and axial FLAIR, axial DWI and ADC mapping weighted sequences were obtained. IV contrast agent was not used.

In sections passing through the infratentorial region, the 4th ventricle is midline and of normal size. Brainstem structures and basal cisterns are within normal limits. Signal intensities of the cerebellar hemispheres are normal.
In sections passing through the supratentorial region, the 3rd and lateral ventricles are midline and of normal size.
Bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Cerebral gray matter structures and sulcal structures are within normal limits. Intracranial herniation was not detected.
In the right centrum semiovale, a hyperintense focus on T2-weighted and FLAIR images showing no diffusion restriction on diffusion-weighted images was observed.

Diffusion restriction was not detected on diffusion-weighted images. No signal change was recorded on the SWI sequence.

Impression: Millimetric hyperintense focus in the right centrum semiovale

report:
BRAIN MRI
SE T1 in axial and sagittal planes, TSE T2 in axial and coronal planes, FLAIR in axial plane, and DWI and ADC mapping in axial plane weighted sequences were obtained. IV contrast agent was not used.

In slices passing through the infratentorial region, the 4th ventricle is midline and of normal width. Brainstem structures and basal cisterns are within normal limits. Signal intensities of the cerebellar hemispheres are normal.
In slices passing through the supratentorial region, the 3rd and lateral ventricles are midline and of normal width.
Bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Cerebral gray matter structures and sulcal structures are within normal limits. Intracranial herniation was not detected.
Several millimetric foci showing subtle hyperintense characteristics on T2-weighted and FLAIR images in the bilateral periventricular white matter and centrum semiovale, showing no diffusion restriction on diffusion-weighted images, were observed.
Dense fluid content was observed in the right frontal sinus. The right middle turbinate is bullous. The nasal septum deviates to the left. Turbinates appear hypertrophic.
No signal change was recorded on SWI sequence.

Impression:
Several millimetric foci with subtle hyperintensity in the bilateral periventricular white matter and centrum semiovale
Right frontal sinusitis, bullous right middle turbinate, turbinate hypertrophy, leftward deviation of the nasal septum

        Cerebellar hemispheres signal distributions appear normal and cerebellar cortical sulci widths are normal. Medulla oblongata, pons, and midbrain morphology and signal intensities are normal. The fourth ventricle is in normal form and location. Prepontine and cerebellopontine cisterns are patent. Signal distributions of the cisternal and canalicular segments of the 7-8 cranial nerve complexes are normal.

        In the supratentorial area, midline structures are in normal location and ventricular system width and morphology are normal. Signal distributions of bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Gray-white matter signal distribution in supratentorial brain parenchyma is normal. Cortical sulcal widths are normal. Corpus callosum morphology and signal intensity are normal. Pathologic contrast enhancement was not detected in infra- and supratentorial brain parenchyma.

        Paranasal sinus aeration is normal. Bilateral globes and retrobulbar fat planes appear normal. Optic chiasm and optic nerve morphology and signal intensities are normal. Flow signal patterns of intracranial major vascular structures were observed as normal. No pathologic significant signal change was detected in the calvarium.

IMPRESSION:
*Contrast-enhanced cranial MRI examination within normal limits.

report:
BRAIN AND CERVICAL VERTEBRA MRI
Clinical information: Head and left arm pain.
Technique: 
Cranial: 3-plane T1A, FLAIR; axial T2A, SWI, and DWI. 
Spinal: Sagittal T1A and T2A; axial T2A. 
Findings:
Cranial MRI:
Brainstem and cerebellum, basal ganglia, internal capsules, and corpus callosum are normal.
There is an increase in bilateral cerebral cystic perivascular widening, and cerebral parenchyma is normal.
Ventricles and sulci are normal.
No specific findings on DWI and SWI sections.
Major cerebral vascular structures are patent and normal.
Right maxillary retention cyst; left maxillary secretions are present. Paranasal sinuses and middle ear aeration, nasopharynx, and orbits are normal.

Cervical spinal MRI:
Cervical vertebral alignment is normal.
C5-6 disc space is narrowed, and there are posterior osteophytes accompanying a broad-based chronic herniation. On axial slice 11 and sagittal T1A slice 12, a newly added left posterior and foraminal herniation is present. The herniation extends beyond osteophyte contours and narrows the left foramen.
Other vertebrae, intervertebral discs, and foramina are normal. There is a perineural cyst in the right C6-7 foramen.
Spinal cord size and signal are normal.
No specific findings in the paravertebral areas.

Impression:
•	Cranial MRI findings within normal limits.

•	C5-6 posterior disc-osteophyte complex; newly added left posterior and foraminal herniation.

PATIENT NAME: [patient_1]
EXAMINATION DATE: [date_1]
EXAMINATION AREA: Contrast-Enhanced Cranial MRI Examination
Protocol No: [accession_1]

TECHNIQUE:
- Sagittal plane 5 mm slice thickness FSE T2, axial plane 5 mm slice thickness FSE T1 and T2, diffusion series, coronal plane 5 mm slice thickness FLAIR weighted slices were obtained. After contrast injection, axial 3D FSE gradient T1, axial 1 mm thickness 3D FSE gradient T1 and coronal 1 mm thickness fat-sat 3D FSE gradient T1 weighted slices were added (15 cc Clariscan was used).

FINDINGS:
    Nasopharynx appears normal, and parafaryngeal fat planes are open. Morphology and signal intensities of the parotid glands entering the slice plane are normal. Clival signal appears normal. Sellar and parasellar structures are normal. No craniocervical junction anomaly was detected.

    Signal distributions of the cerebellar hemispheres appear normal, and widths of cerebellar cortical sulci are normal. Medulla oblongata, pons, and midbrain morphology and signal intensities are normal. The 4th ventricle is in normal form and location. Prepontine and cerebellopontine cisterns are open. Signal distributions of the cisternal and canalicular segments of the 7th-8th cranial nerve complexes are normal.

    Midline structures in the supratentorial area are in normal location, and width and morphology of the ventricular system are normal. Signal distributions of bilateral basal ganglia and thalami are normal. Gray-white matter signal distribution in supratentorial brain parenchyma is normal. Widths of cortical sulci are normal. Corpus callosum morphology and signal intensity are normal.

    No pathological diffusion restriction or pathological contrast enhancement was detected in the neural parenchyma.

    Widespread signal increases in the paranasal sinuses (sinusitis), rudimentary configuration in the frontal sinus, hypertrophy in the conchae, and narrowing in both nasal passages are noted. Bilateral globes and retrobulbar fat planes appear normal. Morphology and signal intensities of the optic chiasm and optic nerves are normal. Flow signal patterns of intracranial major vascular structures appear normal. No pathologically significant signal change was detected in the calvarium.

IMPRESSION:
*Contrast-enhanced cranial MRI examination within normal limits.
    *Widespread signal increases in the paranasal sinuses (sinusitis), rudimentary configuration in the frontal sinus, hypertrophy in the conchae, and narrowing in both nasal passages are noted.

report:
CONTRAST-ENHANCED BRAIN MRI AND MR VENOGRAPHY
Clinical: History of dural sinus thrombosis. Recurrent headache.
Technique: 3-plane T1A, FLAIR; axial T2A, SWI, and DWI. T1A repeat after IV 10 ml Gadovist injection. MR Venography with phase contrast technique.
Comparison: [date_1] Cranial MRI and MR Venography.
Findings:
Upon evaluation comparing the patient's examinations;
•	Brainstem, cerebellum, and cerebral parenchymal signal; ventricles and sulci including right lateral ventricular asymmetry are normal. No specific findings on DWI, SWI, and post-contrast sections.

•	Right cingulate gyrus tentorial herniation (axial 77, coronal 128) and right lateral occipital gyrus transverse sinus intrasinus herniation (axial 51, coronal 137) persist.

•	Right vertebral artery is congenitally hypoplastic and other cerebral main arteries are normal.

•	On MR venography and post-contrast sections;

o	Since venography was performed with contrast in the previous examination, arterial and venous diameters are slightly wider compared to the current examination and are visible in cervical sections.

o	Right transverse and sigmoid sinuses are hypoplastic. The sinus narrowing caused by occipital giral herniation and arachnoid granulation distal to the transverse sinus is stable.

o	Hypoplasia of the sigmoid sinus and proximal IJV persists, and the right mastoid emissary vein asymmetrically dilates to provide right venous drainage.

o	No newly formed thrombus in dural sinuses or abnormal vascular development that could be related to AVF was detected.

There is total loss of aeration in the right posterior ethmoid. Left sphenoidal and maxillary retention cysts are present. Other paranasal sinuses and middle ear aeration, nasopharynx, and orbits are normal. No specific findings in visualized bones.

Impression:
•	Cranial MRI findings within normal limits; right posterior ethmoid sinusitis.
•	Stable right sigmoid and transverse sinus hypoplasias. No thrombus development in dural sinuses was detected.

	Bone structures within the field of view and subgaleal tissue are normal.

	In the evaluation of the orbits within the field of view; globes, extraorbital muscles, retrobulbar fat tissue, and optic nerves are evaluated as normal.

Basal ganglia and thalami are normal.
Cortical sulci and gyri are normal.
In slices obtained after contrast agent injection, pathological contrast enhancement was not observed.
Impression: Contrast-enhanced brain MRI findings within normal limits.

report:
CONTRAST-ENHANCED CRANIAL MRI EXAMINATION:
Technique: Axial FSE T2 and FLAIR T2, coronal FSE T1 and sagittal FSE T2 sequences, 3D BRAVO after intravenous contrast administration.

Findings:
	Bone structures and subgaleal tissue within the field of view are normal.
Evaluation of the craniofacial region: Left inferior turbinate hypertrophy is present. Paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells are normal.

	Evaluation of the orbits within the field of view: The globes, extraocular muscles, retrobulbar fat, and optic nerves are evaluated as normal.

In the posterior fossa sequences: A defect thought to be secondary to prior surgery on the left occipital bone is noted. No contrast enhancement suggestive of recurrence or residual disease is seen in this area. The fourth ventricle is normal in location, shape, and size. The cerebellar hemispheres appear symmetric, and gray-white matter differentiation is unremarkable.
	Brainstem structures, pons, and midbrain are unremarkable.
The basal cisterns are patent.

	In the supratentorial sequences: White and gray matter signal intensities are normal in both hemispheres. The ventricular system is midline symmetric and normal in size for age. An appearance suggestive of pituitary microadenoma measuring 4.4 x 5.4 x 6 mm is noted in the posterior and right aspect of the adenohypophysis.
	The basal ganglia and thalami are normal.
	Cortical sulci and gyri are regular.
On post-contrast sequences, no pathological contrast enhancement is noted.

Impression:
	Defect in the left occipital bone thought to be secondary to prior surgery
	Microadenoma in the posterior and right aspect of the adenohypophysis?

report:
CRANIAL MR EXAMINATION:
Technique: Axial FSE T2 and FLAIR T2, coronal FSE T1, and sagittal FSE T2 sequences.

Examination:
    Osseous structures and subgaleal soft tissue within the field of view are normal.
Evaluation of craniofacial structures; Variational pterygoid pneumatization extending from the lateral aspect of the sphenoid sinuses toward the temporal region is noted. Mucoperiosteal thickening is present in the frontal and ethmoid sinuses. The nasal cavity, other paranasal sinuses, and mastoid air cells are normal. Hypertrophy is noted in the inferior turbinates.

    Evaluation of the orbits within the field of view; The globes, extraocular muscles, retrobulbar fat, and optic nerves are evaluated as normal.

Posterior fossa series; The fourth ventricle is in normal location, shape, and size. The cerebellar hemispheres are symmetric, and gray-white matter differentiation is unremarkable.
    Brainstem structures, pons, and midbrain are normal.
Basal cisterns are patent.

    Supratentorial series; White and gray matter signal intensities in both hemispheres are normal. Hyperintense plaques are noted in the periventricular white matter on T2-weighted images (minimal microangiopathic changes?).
The ventricular system is midline symmetric and of width compatible with age.
The basal ganglia and thalami are normal.

CONCLUSION: Minimal microangiopathic changes?

    Basal ganglia and thalami are normal.

        Increased AP diameter of both globes - Funnel-shaped focal convexities near the midline in the right globe (secondary to prior surgery?).

Coronal plane; T2-weighted FSE.

The fourth ventricle is of normal size and configuration. Basal cisterns are open.
Cerebellar hemispheres, vermis, and brainstem are of normal signal intensity and configuration.

A finding consistent with an arachnoid cyst measuring 25x27x18 mm is present at the level of the cisterna magna.

Both internal acoustic canals are of normal width.
The sella is of normal shape. Both cavernous sinuses are symmetric and of normal signal intensity.

Convexity sulci are consistent with the patient's age.
Cerebral white and gray matter signal separation is normal, and no pathological signal change was detected.

The corpus callosum is of normal signal intensity and configuration.
Pericallosal pathology was not detected.

The ventricular system is of normal width, and the third ventricle is midline.
No deviation was observed in the midline structures.

Extra- and intra-axial collections were not detected.
No findings suggestive of thrombus were detected in the dural sinuses.

Aeration of the paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells is normal.

Impression: Appearance consistent with an arachnoid cyst in the cisterna magna
